description_long: This bird features a striking combination of colors, with a black head and face, a white spot near the beak, and a rich orange-brown patch on its breast and flanks. The wings and back are olive-brown with subtle lighter markings, while the tail is long and tapers to a point, displaying a mix of brown and olive hues. The beak is short, straight, and conical, suited for seed eating. The legs are slender and appear dark, blending with the overall earthy tones of the bird. The plumage is smooth and well-kept, with a slight sheen visible on the darker feathers. This bird's size and coloration suggest it is adapted for life in wooded or urban park environments.
description: A small bird with a black head, orange-brown breast, olive-brown wings, and a pointed tail. It has a white spot near the beak and a smooth, conical bill.
description_view_0: Side view showing the black head, white spot near the beak, orange-brown breast, and long olive-brown tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of olive-brown wings and the pointed tail feathers.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the black head, orange-brown breast, and the full length of the tail.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the black head, conical beak, and the smooth transition from dark head to lighter brown back and tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid black head, orange-brown breast and flanks, olive-brown wings and back, with a white spot near the beak
